metadata.stageDescription: 仓库当前以架构文档、契约文档和工程骨架为主，真实业务实现仍集中在少数基础模块。
summaryBullets: 根聚合工程可以通过 `mvn -q test`，说明 Maven 多模块骨架和最小测试链路已打通。
HJO2OA-Content、HJO2OA-Collaboration、HJO2OA-DataServices 目前基本仍是文档与 POM 骨架。
frontend 仅存在 apps/packages/public 目录骨架，尚无可运行应用。
自动化开发更适合从单模块、强契约、低外部依赖的切片切入。
globalConstraints: 遵守 Java 17、Spring Boot 3.3.x、Maven 3.9+ 基线。
优先在单模块内完成闭环，跨模块写联动通过事件完成。
禁止直接依赖其他业务模块的持久化实现或越权写入其他模块主数据。
优先补齐最小可验证骨架，再扩展真实持久化、缓存和消息适配。
每个开发任务完成后至少执行其模块测试或根 `mvn -q test`。

tasks.title: 补齐共享 Web 响应体与全局异常骨架
tasks.goal: 在共享层建立统一响应体、基础业务异常和全局异常处理骨架，为后续业务模块暴露 REST API 提供一致的返回约定。
tasks.scope: 仅改动 HJO2OA-Shared 下的 shared.web / shared.kernel 等包，不扩展具体业务规则。
tasks.whyNow: 当前仓库已经开始出现 Controller，但还没有统一的 Web 契约实现；先统一横切层可以降低后续模块重复建设。
acceptanceCriteria: 提供统一响应对象（例如 success/error/meta）和基础分页响应模型。
提供可复用的业务异常基类以及映射到 HTTP 状态码的全局异常处理器。
至少补充 1 组单元测试或 MVC 切片测试，证明异常到响应体的映射可用。
不在共享层引入具体业务错误码枚举，只保留可扩展抽象。

tasks.title: 补齐 identity-context 最小会话骨架
tasks.goal: 在现有 refresh 事件发布能力基础上，补齐 current、available、switch、reset-primary 四类最小会话接口，并保留后续接入缓存/数据库的抽象边界。
tasks.scope: 优先实现 application/domain/interfaces 的最小闭环，可先使用可替换的内存级仓储或 provider，暂不接真实组织主数据持久化。
tasks.whyNow: identity-context 已具备真实事件和测试，是当前最接近业务实现的模块，也是一期其它模块的前置依赖。
acceptanceCriteria: 新增 current / available / switch / reset-primary 的 application service 与 REST 接口骨架。
切换逻辑明确区分主岗与兼岗，并对无效目标返回稳定错误。
refresh 与 switch 共用一致的上下文表示，避免接口口径漂移。
至少补充 2 组测试，覆盖成功切换和非法切换场景。

tasks.title: 搭建 todo-center 事件投影与查询骨架
tasks.goal: 围绕 `process.task.*` 事件建立待办投影抽象、幂等更新入口和基础查询接口，为门户工作台与消息提醒提供统一待办视图。
tasks.scope: 先完成事件对象、投影模型、repository 抽象和最小列表查询接口；不实现完整流程审批写操作。
tasks.whyNow: todo-center 是一期闭环中的关键读模型，且其契约文档已经比较完整，适合在 identity-context 之后率先落地。
acceptanceCriteria: 定义 todo 投影模型、状态枚举和 repository 抽象。
提供 process.task.created / completed / terminated / transferred 的消费入口骨架与幂等键约定。
提供至少 1 个待办列表查询接口或 facade，明确受当前 identity-context 约束。
补充单元测试覆盖重复事件不产生重复投影。

tasks.title: 建立门户首页聚合读模型骨架
tasks.goal: 建立仅面向门户首页的聚合读模型接口骨架，消费 identity-context、todo 统计和消息摘要，而不是承担开放 API 职责。
tasks.scope: 先完成只读 facade、缓存 key 约定和首页 DTO，不直接写其他模块主数据。
tasks.whyNow: 门户聚合层是一期用户侧闭环的关键读入口，但必须建立在身份上下文和待办读模型之上。
acceptanceCriteria: 聚合接口明确只读边界，不暴露通用开放 API。
首页 DTO 至少包含 identity、todo 统计和消息摘要三个区块。
缓存 key 设计包含 tenantId、personId、assignmentId 或等价上下文字段。
补充单元测试验证聚合服务对空数据和部分缺失数据的容错行为。

tasks.title: 建立消息中心待办提醒消费骨架
tasks.goal: 消费 todo 与流程超时事件，建立站内消息与送达记录的最小模型，保持消息中心与渠道发送层解耦。
tasks.scope: 先落 message aggregate、delivery record 抽象和事件监听入口，不接短信/邮件等外部渠道。
tasks.whyNow: 消息模块的一期职责是触达而不是持有待办主数据，因此最合理的起点是从 todo 事件消费开始。
acceptanceCriteria: 消息中心消费 `todo.item.created` 和 `todo.item.overdue` 的监听骨架可用。
消息模型与渠道适配器分层清晰，不在同一类中混写。
送达记录保留可扩展状态机，但一期只实现站内消息。
至少补充 1 组测试证明重复事件不会生成重复消息记录。

tasks.title: 补齐本地联调环境模板
tasks.goal: 为 SQL Server、Redis、RabbitMQ、MinIO 提供本地联调模板，明确 profile 用法和缺省配置来源，降低后续模块开发成本。
tasks.scope: 优先补齐 `application-local.yml` 说明文档、环境变量示例或容器编排模板，不扩展生产部署编排。
tasks.whyNow: 当前后端配置已经声明多个外部基础设施，但仓库里还没有统一的本地联调入口说明。
acceptanceCriteria: 提供清晰的本地联调说明或模板文件，覆盖 SQL Server、Redis、RabbitMQ、MinIO。
明确 Spring profile 的推荐启动方式和必要环境变量。
不改动现有默认端口与核心配置命名约定。
验证根应用可以在不连接真实外部服务的前提下完成基础启动或测试。

tasks.title: 建立 portal-home 页面装配骨架
tasks.goal: 建立门户首页、办公中心和移动工作台的最小页面装配层，只消费聚合卡片快照和静态模板结果，不直连业务模块。
tasks.scope: 优先实现 scene/layout/region/card 的只读装配模型、静态模板 provider 和最小 REST 接口；暂不引入真实 portal-model 与 personalization 持久化。
tasks.whyNow: aggregation-api 已经具备首页三块读模型骨架，继续补 portal-home 可以把门户域推进到真正用户入口，而不是停留在内部聚合层。
acceptanceCriteria: 提供 HOME / OFFICE_CENTER / MOBILE_WORKBENCH 三类 scene 的页面装配 DTO 和应用服务。
portal-home 只通过 provider 消费 aggregation-api，不直连 todo-center、message-center、identity-context。
暴露最小只读页面接口并接入共享 Web 契约。
补充测试覆盖页面装配、局部降级和控制器响应契约。

tasks.title: 补 aggregation-api 快照状态与失效监听骨架
tasks.goal: 把 aggregation-api 的快照语义从当前的最小缓存提升到 READY / STALE / FAILED 与事件驱动失效骨架，支撑 portal-home 后续定向刷新。
tasks.scope: 优先补齐快照状态模型、失效入口、最小监听器和测试；暂不接真实 Redis 或跨进程通知。
tasks.whyNow: portal-home 已经依赖聚合快照做页面装配，下一步最有价值的是让快照具备可失效、可降级、可刷新边界，而不是继续扩张前端外观层。
acceptanceCriteria: 快照状态至少覆盖 READY / STALE / FAILED 并有明确回退语义。
提供最小失效入口，可按 identity 或 cardType 定向标记快照过期。
消费至少一类门户相关事件完成失效骨架联动。
补充测试覆盖过期标记、失败降级和定向刷新入口。

tasks.title: 补 aggregation-api 快照刷新结果事件出口
tasks.goal: 把 aggregation-api 已声明的 `portal.snapshot.refreshed` 与 `portal.snapshot.failed` 变成真实可发布事件，支撑 portal-home 轮询刷新和诊断链路。
tasks.scope: 仅补快照刷新成功/失败事件模型、应用服务发布点与测试；不引入跨进程推送或前端订阅。
tasks.whyNow: 快照状态和失效语义已经具备，但没有刷新结果事件时，下游只能被动重拉，无法区分已刷新与刷新失败。
acceptanceCriteria: 首次回源生成 READY 快照时发布 `portal.snapshot.refreshed`。
回源失败生成 FAILED 快照时发布 `portal.snapshot.failed`。
纯缓存命中不重复发布刷新结果事件。
补充测试覆盖成功发布、失败发布和 refresh endpoint 二次刷新。

tasks.title: 把跨模块 DomainEvent 监听骨架接入 Spring 事件总线
tasks.goal: 让 todo-center、message-center、aggregation-api 中已经存在的 listener 骨架真正消费 Spring ApplicationEvent，避免事件链路只在单元测试中成立。
tasks.scope: 仅补 @EventListener 绑定和运行时分发测试，不引入外部消息中间件或异步消费器。
tasks.whyNow: 当前各模块已经通过 DomainEventPublisher 发布事件，但 listener 方法没有挂接到 Spring 事件总线，实际运行时链路是断的。
acceptanceCriteria: TodoProcessEventListener、TodoReminderEventListener、PortalSnapshotInvalidationListener 都通过 Spring 事件机制触发。
补充事件绑定测试，证明 publishEvent 后会进入对应应用服务。
不改变既有应用服务业务语义，只修正运行时接线。

tasks.title: 补 message-center 单条已读闭环
tasks.goal: 把 message-center 从只读查询推进到最小用户动作闭环，支持单条消息已读、审计记录与 msg.notification.read 事件发布。
tasks.scope: 仅实现单条已读动作、已读审计、读事件和门户消息卡片失效联动；不扩批量已读、归档或撤回。
tasks.whyNow: 门户消息卡片已经能展示未读数和最新消息，但没有已读动作就无法形成真实消费闭环，也无法产出 msg.notification.read 事件源。
acceptanceCriteria: 新增单条已读应用服务和 POST /api/v1/msg/messages/{notificationId}/read 接口。
首次已读写入审计记录并发布 msg.notification.read；重复已读幂等。
aggregation-api 消费 msg.notification.read 后标记消息卡片快照过期。
补充测试覆盖已读成功、幂等重复读和门户失效联动。

tasks.title: 补 aggregation-api 对 STALE 快照的按需重建协调
tasks.goal: 让 aggregation-api 在返回 STALE 快照时具备明确的按需重建策略，而不是只返回旧值不再推进刷新。
tasks.scope: 优先实现最小协调器、定向刷新入口复用和测试；暂不引入 Redis 分布式锁或后台任务编排。
tasks.whyNow: 消息已读和现有业务事件都已经能把快照打成 STALE，但读路径还没有真正的重建协调，事件失效链路仍只完成了一半。
acceptanceCriteria: STALE 快照在 dashboard/card 读取时具备明确的重建或后台刷新策略。
不重复刷新 READY 快照，避免无意义回源。
补充测试覆盖 STALE 命中后的重建行为和事件发布。

tasks.title: 补 message-center 单条消息详情查询
tasks.goal: 为消息中心补齐单条消息详情读取能力，统一复用当前身份可见性校验、来源快照和消息状态语义，支撑办公中心右侧详情区和后续批量动作前的单项查看。
tasks.scope: 优先实现 GET /api/v1/msg/messages/{notificationId}、最小详情 DTO 和测试；暂不实现归档、撤回或过期动作。
tasks.whyNow: message-center 已经具备列表、未读汇总和单条已读动作，缺少详情读取时办公中心只能看到摘要，无法继续推进消息页闭环。
acceptanceCriteria: 新增 GET /api/v1/msg/messages/{notificationId} 接口和最小详情 DTO。
详情读取只允许访问当前身份可见消息，不暴露跨身份或跨租户数据。
详情至少返回 sourceEventType、sourceBusinessId、readAt、expiredAt 等文档已声明的核心字段。
补充测试覆盖可见详情读取和无权/不存在时的 404。

tasks.title: 补 todo-center 已办列表最小闭环
tasks.goal: 在当前待办投影骨架上补已办列表查询，让门户和办公中心具备 pending/completed 的最小双视图，而不引入审批写动作。
tasks.scope: 优先实现 completed 列表的 query/service/controller 与测试；暂不扩复杂筛选和分页元数据。
tasks.whyNow: 办公中心只靠 pending 无法形成完整事务处理视图，completed 是下一阶段列表聚合最必要的下游读模型。
acceptanceCriteria: 新增 completed 列表查询入口，受当前 identity-context 约束。
已办列表按完成时间稳定排序。
不改变现有待办投影写路径，只复用已完成状态数据。
补充测试覆盖 completed 可见性和排序。

tasks.title: 补 todo-center 超时投影与 todo.item.overdue 出口
tasks.goal: 消费流程超时事件并在待办中心形成超时视图与 overdue 领域事件，为消息提醒和门户紧急卡片提供真实源头。
tasks.scope: 优先补最小超时状态、事件出口和测试；暂不实现调度器或 SLA 配置。
tasks.whyNow: message-center 已有 overdue 提醒投影入口，但上游还没有稳定的 overdue 事件真相源。
acceptanceCriteria: 新增超时投影更新逻辑并发布 todo.item.overdue。
重复超时事件保持幂等，不重复发布提醒源事件。
超时数量可被后续聚合层读取。
补充测试覆盖首次超时与重复超时。

tasks.title: 补 message-center 批量已读最小闭环
tasks.goal: 在单条已读闭环之上补批量已读入口，返回部分成功/失败结果，复用单条已读的幂等、审计和事件模型。
tasks.scope: 仅实现 bulk-read 动作和测试；暂不扩展批量归档或撤回。
tasks.whyNow: 办公中心消息页需要在列表视角下执行批量已读，否则用户操作成本过高。
acceptanceCriteria: 新增批量已读接口，限制最大批次大小。
逐条幂等处理，允许部分成功而不做整体回滚。
复用现有 read 审计和 msg.notification.read 事件语义。
补充测试覆盖部分成功和重复已读。

tasks.title: 补 todo-center 抄送列表与已阅标记
tasks.goal: 为待办中心补抄送列表与已阅动作，让事务读模型从 pending/completed 继续扩到 copied 视角。
tasks.scope: 优先实现 copied 列表和 copied read 动作；暂不扩展草稿或我发起。
tasks.whyNow: 办公中心要形成更完整的工作流事务入口，copied 是成本最低的第三视图。
acceptanceCriteria: 新增 copied 列表查询与 copied read 动作。
已阅动作天然幂等，不影响待办/已办主状态。
列表继续受当前 identity-context 约束。
补充测试覆盖 copied 可见性和重复已阅。

tasks.title: 补 message-center 超时待办催办提醒
tasks.goal: 消费 todo.item.overdue 生成站内催办提醒，扩完整个消息中心从 created/read 到 overdue 的主链路。
tasks.scope: 仅实现 overdue 提醒消费、幂等和测试；不接外部渠道。
tasks.whyNow: 当前消息提醒主线只覆盖 created/read，缺少 overdue 时门户紧急感知仍是空的。
acceptanceCriteria: 消费 todo.item.overdue 并生成 TODO_OVERDUE 站内提醒。
重复 overdue 事件保持幂等，不重复落消息。
继续发布 msg.notification.sent 供门户快照失效。
补充测试覆盖首次 overdue 与重复 overdue。

tasks.title: 建立办公中心聚合读模型骨架
tasks.goal: 在 aggregation-api 与 portal-home 之间补办公中心场景的只读聚合骨架，先整合 TODO 和 MESSAGE 两块。
tasks.scope: 优先实现 office-center DTO、application service 和最小接口；不扩成通用开放 API。
tasks.whyNow: 门户首页读模型已经成形，办公中心是下一个自然落点，但需要已办和消息详情两个下游基础能力先到位。
acceptanceCriteria: 新增办公中心聚合 DTO 和最小读接口。
至少聚合 todo 和 message 两块，保持只读边界。
portal-home 可消费该聚合结果而不直连业务模块。
补充测试覆盖空数据与局部降级。

tasks.title: 补门户待办列表卡片分页查询
tasks.goal: 在 aggregation-api 暴露可直接供办公中心消费的待办列表卡片查询，聚合 pending/completed/copied 三类最小视图。
tasks.scope: 优先实现分页读取和最小筛选；不扩展写动作。
tasks.whyNow: 办公中心骨架落地后，待办区域最先需要从计数卡片推进到真实列表。
acceptanceCriteria: 新增待办列表分页查询接口和 DTO。
可选择 pending/completed/copied 最小视图。
保持聚合层不越权写入 todo-center 主数据。
补充测试覆盖分页与空结果。

tasks.title: 补门户消息列表卡片分页查询
tasks.goal: 在 aggregation-api 补消息列表分页和未读摘要刷新入口，让办公中心能通过聚合层拿到列表加动作后的刷新结果。
tasks.scope: 优先实现消息列表分页、未读摘要和最小筛选；不扩外部推送。
tasks.whyNow: 消息中心已有 created/read/overdue 主链路后，办公中心才值得接真实的消息列表卡片。
acceptanceCriteria: 新增消息列表分页查询接口和 DTO。
列表可反映 read/bulk-read/reminder 后的最新未读状态。
不在聚合层持有消息真相源，只读消费 message-center。
补充测试覆盖分页、空结果和未读摘要刷新。

tasks.title: 稳定 identity-context refresh 结果语义
tasks.goal: 为 identity-context 的 refresh 结果补稳定语义，明确区分已恢复、回退主岗和必须重新登录三类结果，避免下游靠异常猜测状态。
tasks.scope: 优先改 application/domain 结果模型和测试；暂不接真实登录体系。
tasks.whyNow: 后续门户与移动端会更依赖 refresh 结果做用户态恢复，这条契约需要在更复杂场景前先稳定下来。
acceptanceCriteria: refresh 返回结果能表达恢复成功、回退主岗和必须重新登录。
不依赖异常文本让下游判断会话状态。
保持现有事件发布边界不被破坏。
补充测试覆盖三类 refresh 结果。

tasks.title: Lock identity-context refresh HTTP contract
tasks.goal: Freeze the refresh outcome semantics at the HTTP layer so downstream clients can rely on the API response instead of service-only tests.
tasks.scope: Add controller MockMvc coverage and request validation coverage without introducing real auth or gateway integration.
tasks.whyNow: Portal and mobile flows consume the HTTP response shape, so the explicit refresh outcome must be stable there before more consumers are added.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/org-perm/identity-context/refresh returns outcome values RECOVERED, FALLBACK_TO_PRIMARY, and RELOGIN_REQUIRED.
forceLogout=true returns currentContext as null in the HTTP response.
MockMvc covers request validation failure for refresh.
Tests stay local to the module and do not require external infrastructure.

tasks.title: Add upstream invalidation listeners for identity-context
tasks.goal: Let identity-context consume upstream org assignment, position, person, and account invalidation events and route them into the unified refresh flow.
tasks.scope: Implement local listeners, minimal event DTO adapters, and tests without wiring real cross-module publishers.
tasks.whyNow: The refresh outcome is now stable, and the next missing boundary is the upstream event input side described in the module contracts.
acceptanceCriteria: Listeners consume assignment removed or expired, position disabled, person disabled, and account locked events.
Listeners map all supported cases into RefreshIdentityContextCommand instead of duplicating fallback logic.
Tests cover fallback versus force-logout behavior.
Binding tests verify @EventListener wiring.

tasks.title: Add portal config-driven snapshot invalidation
tasks.goal: Make aggregation-api consume publication and personalization events so portal snapshots are invalidated when page structure or personalization changes.
tasks.scope: Add listeners, invalidation mapping, and tests without integrating real portal-model or personalization modules.
tasks.whyNow: Snapshot state and invalidation already exist, but the config-driven invalidation branch described in the docs is still missing.
acceptanceCriteria: Listeners consume publication activated or offlined and personalization saved or reset events.
Affected portal snapshots are marked STALE instead of being hard-deleted.
AggregationApi remains read-side only and does not become a portal-model source of truth.
Tests cover event binding and invalidation scope.

tasks.title: Add portal-home snapshot event consumption
tasks.goal: Let portal-home consume snapshot refreshed and failed events so the page assembly layer can expose minimal refresh feedback instead of ignoring those events.
tasks.scope: Add listeners, a minimal in-memory state holder, and tests without implementing frontend push.
tasks.whyNow: AggregationApi already publishes snapshot events, but the portal-home side of that chain is still missing.
acceptanceCriteria: Listeners consume portal.snapshot.refreshed and portal.snapshot.failed.
Portal-home keeps minimal page-level refresh diagnostics or status.
Portal-home does not take ownership of aggregation snapshot truth.
Tests cover both refreshed and failed branches.

tasks.title: Add widget-driven snapshot invalidation
tasks.goal: Make aggregation-api consume portal.widget.updated and portal.widget.disabled so card-level snapshot invalidation follows widget definition changes.
tasks.scope: Add lightweight widget event DTOs, listener mapping, and tests without implementing widget-config persistence or designer write paths.
tasks.whyNow: Publication and personalization changes already invalidate snapshots, but widget definition changes would still leave stale card snapshots cached.
acceptanceCriteria: Listeners consume portal.widget.updated and portal.widget.disabled.
Widget events invalidate only the affected card type instead of all cards.
Scene-scoped widget events only invalidate matching scenes, while null sceneType can invalidate all tenant scenes.
Tests cover both direct listener behavior and Spring event binding.

tasks.title: Add portal-home widget event consumption
tasks.goal: Let portal-home treat widget updates and disables as reload-required refresh-state signals without taking ownership of widget truth.
tasks.scope: Extend the existing refresh-state listener and tests without adding realtime push or frontend persistence.
tasks.whyNow: Portal-home already consumes snapshot, publication, and personalization events; widget changes are the next missing refresh-state input.
acceptanceCriteria: portal-home consumes portal.widget.updated and portal.widget.disabled.
Scene-scoped widget events mark only the matching scene as RELOAD_REQUIRED.
Null-scoped widget disable events mark all scenes as RELOAD_REQUIRED.
Tests cover both target-scene and all-scene branches.

tasks.title: Expose portal-home refresh-state polling endpoint
tasks.goal: Expose the current portal-home refresh state through a lightweight read endpoint so callers can poll refresh diagnostics without reassembling the whole page.
tasks.scope: Extend the existing controller and MockMvc tests without adding auth, push channels, or new storage.
tasks.whyNow: The page response already embeds refresh state, but polling the whole page is wasteful once the sidecar state exists independently.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/portal/home/refresh-state returns the current scene refresh state.
The endpoint uses the shared web contract.
The response can expose non-IDLE states produced by the refresh-state application service.
MockMvc covers a non-IDLE response branch.

tasks.title: Route OFFICE_CENTER page assembly through office-center aggregation
tasks.goal: Let the OFFICE_CENTER scene consume the dedicated office-center aggregate instead of only the generic dashboard snapshot, so page navigation and summary areas can reflect office-specific counts.
tasks.scope: Add a narrow provider or assembly extension plus tests without redesigning the full page model or implementing frontend widgets.
tasks.whyNow: Aggregation-api already has an office-center aggregate, but portal-home still assembles OFFICE_CENTER from the generic dashboard-only provider.
acceptanceCriteria: OFFICE_CENTER assembly consumes office-center aggregate data through a portal-home boundary provider.
Office navigation or summary data reflects dynamic counts instead of only static template links.
HOME and MOBILE_WORKBENCH flows keep their existing assembly path.
Tests cover the office-center-specific assembly branch.

tasks.title: Add dynamic badge counts to mobile workbench navigation
tasks.goal: Use the existing dashboard snapshot to expose todo and message counts in the MOBILE_WORKBENCH navigation so the mobile scene reflects current workload at a glance.
tasks.scope: Extend page assembly and tests using the existing page DTOs and snapshot data without introducing new providers or frontend push.
tasks.whyNow: Navigation badges were introduced for office-center, and mobile can reuse the same page contract with lower implementation cost because the required counts already exist in dashboard snapshots.
acceptanceCriteria: MOBILE_WORKBENCH navigation exposes todo and message badge counts.
Badge counts come from the current dashboard snapshot rather than static template values.
HOME and OFFICE_CENTER navigation behavior remains stable.
Tests cover page assembly and controller response for the mobile scene.

tasks.title: Build personalization save/reset skeleton and event output
tasks.goal: Implement a minimal personalization profile save/reset flow that emits portal.personalization.saved and portal.personalization.reset from the source module instead of only consuming those events downstream.
tasks.scope: Add a lightweight profile model, in-memory repository, application service, REST API, and tests without implementing full drag-drop layout editing or persistent storage.
tasks.whyNow: Aggregation-api and portal-home already consume personalization events, but the source module that should produce them is still an empty skeleton.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide save and reset application services plus REST endpoints for a minimal personalization profile.
Saving emits portal.personalization.saved and reset emits portal.personalization.reset.
The module keeps portal-model as the template source of truth and only stores limited user overrides.
Tests cover save, reset, and event publication without external infrastructure.

tasks.title: Build widget-config update/disable source skeleton and event output
tasks.goal: Implement a minimal widget definition update/disable flow that emits portal.widget.updated and portal.widget.disabled from the source module instead of only consuming those events downstream.
tasks.scope: Add a lightweight widget definition model, in-memory repository, application service, REST API, and tests without building a full schema designer or persistence layer.
tasks.whyNow: Aggregation-api and portal-home already consume widget events, but the widget-config module that should produce them is still an empty skeleton.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide update and disable application services plus REST endpoints for a minimal widget definition.
Updating emits portal.widget.updated and disabling emits portal.widget.disabled.
The module stores only source-side widget metadata needed by current portal consumers.
Tests cover update, disable, and event publication without external infrastructure.

tasks.title: Build portal-model publication activation/offline source skeleton and event output
tasks.goal: Implement a minimal publication activation/offline flow that emits portal.publication.activated and portal.publication.offlined from the source module instead of only consuming those events downstream.
tasks.scope: Add a lightweight publication model, in-memory repository, application service, REST API, and tests without building full template version management or scheduling.
tasks.whyNow: personalization, aggregation-api, and portal-home already consume publication events, but the portal-model source module still has no runnable implementation to produce them.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide activation and offline application services plus REST endpoints for a minimal portal publication.
Activation emits portal.publication.activated and offlining emits portal.publication.offlined.
The module stores only source-side publication state needed by current portal consumers.
Tests cover activation, offline, and event publication without external infrastructure.

tasks.title: Add personalization publication listener and mutable base publication resolver
tasks.goal: Make personalization react to portal-model publication activation/offline events so base publication bindings stop relying on fixed hard-coded defaults.
tasks.scope: Introduce a lightweight mutable base publication resolver, add publication event listeners, and cover the new behavior with focused tests without implementing full template parsing.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model can now emit publication source events, but personalization still resolves basePublicationId from a static scene switch and cannot follow source-side publication changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Personalization consumes portal.publication.activated and updates the scene base publication binding.
Personalization consumes portal.publication.offlined and clears or resets affected scene bindings safely.
Current/read, save, and reset flows continue to resolve basePublicationId without external infrastructure.
Tests cover resolver updates and event listener behavior.

tasks.title: Add portal-model widget reference invalidation listener skeleton
tasks.goal: Make portal-model consume widget-config update and disable events so template-side widget reference status can be invalidated from source-side widget changes.
tasks.scope: Add a lightweight in-memory reference status projection or invalidation tracker, event listeners, and tests without implementing full template/page/version persistence.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model event contracts already declare widget-config as an upstream event source, but the module still has no runtime behavior when widget definitions change or are disabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-model consumes portal.widget.updated and portal.widget.disabled events from widget-config.
Widget change events update an in-memory invalidation/status projection keyed by widgetId or affected scope.
The listener does not rewrite template structure automatically; it only updates reference-status side effects.
Tests cover event dispatch and invalidation behavior without external infrastructure.

tasks.title: Expose current active portal publication query from source module
tasks.goal: Provide a lightweight source-side query for the currently active portal publication by scene and client type so downstream modules can stop depending only on hard-coded publication IDs.
tasks.scope: Add query service and REST read endpoint over the existing in-memory publication repository without implementing audience matching, template parsing, or page assembly.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model can already activate/offline publications and downstream modules now react to publication events, but there is still no source-side read API for the active publication state.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-model exposes a query for the current active publication by sceneType and clientType.
Offlined publications are no longer returned by the active publication query.
The query reuses the existing source-side in-memory repository and shared web contract.
Tests cover the query service and endpoint behavior without external infrastructure.

tasks.title: Expose portal-model widget reference status query from source projection
tasks.goal: Provide a lightweight read endpoint for the widget reference invalidation projection so downstream tools can inspect whether a widget change left template references stale or requiring repair.
tasks.scope: Expose the existing in-memory widget reference status projection by widgetId through a shared-web-contract REST endpoint without implementing template editing or batch search.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model already consumes widget-config events and keeps a projection, but there was still no source-side way to read that projection.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-model exposes the current widget reference status by widgetId.
The endpoint returns RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND when no projection exists for the widget.
The query reuses the existing in-memory projection and shared web contract.
Tests cover both successful reads and missing-status cases.

tasks.title: Bootstrap personalization base publication from portal-model active query
tasks.goal: Make personalization resolve its base publication from the portal-model active publication query so it can recover source truth after startup or missed events instead of relying only on seeded default IDs.
tasks.scope: Introduce a lightweight active publication provider and wire it into the mutable base publication resolver or read path without implementing remote HTTP clients or full template parsing.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model now exposes the current active publication state, and personalization already reacts to publication events, so the remaining gap is source-driven bootstrap and resynchronization.
acceptanceCriteria: Personalization can resolve basePublicationId from portal-model active publication state when no local override is bound.
Event-driven updates continue to work and override bootstrap state when newer source events arrive.
Current/read, save, and reset flows still work without external infrastructure.
Tests cover bootstrap resolution and fallback behavior.

tasks.title: Build portal-model template create/publish/deprecate source skeleton
tasks.goal: Implement a minimal template and version lifecycle in portal-model so the module can publish portal.template.created, portal.template.published, and portal.template.deprecated from source state instead of treating templateId as a write-only placeholder.
tasks.scope: Add lightweight template/version domain models, in-memory repository, application service, REST API, and tests without implementing page trees, region editing, preview rendering, or audience resolution.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model already owns publication source events, but template lifecycle events and source-side version state are still missing, which blocks designer-side timeline and published-baseline behaviors.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-model provides minimal create, publish, and deprecate commands for template source state.
Template create publishes portal.template.created, publish emits portal.template.published, and deprecate emits portal.template.deprecated.
The module keeps template version state in its own in-memory repository and can query the current template record.
Tests cover lifecycle transitions, duplicate template code rejection, and controller behavior without external infrastructure.

tasks.title: Add portal-designer template timeline and publication status projection
tasks.goal: Make portal-designer consume template and publication source events so it can expose a minimal version timeline and online publication status panel instead of remaining an empty shell.
tasks.scope: Add lightweight in-memory projection models, event listeners, read application service, and tests without implementing canvas editing, multi-user collaboration, or preview rendering.
tasks.whyNow: portal-designer is currently only package placeholders, and the newly added portal-model source events provide exactly the minimal upstream data needed for a designer status view.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-designer consumes portal.template.created, portal.template.published, and portal.template.deprecated into a template timeline projection.
Portal-designer consumes portal.publication.activated and portal.publication.offlined into a publication status projection.
A lightweight read API or application service can return the current timeline and online status for a template.
Tests cover event dispatch, repeated updates, and read behavior without external infrastructure.

tasks.title: Route portal-home template provider through portal-model source resolution
tasks.goal: Replace portal-home's static page template dependency with a source-backed template provider that resolves the active publication and current template metadata from portal-model before applying scene-specific assembly.
tasks.scope: Add a narrow provider and tests that bridge portal-home to portal-model source queries without implementing full page/region modeling or frontend rendering changes.
tasks.whyNow: portal-home still assembles pages from hard-coded templates even though portal-model now owns publication state and will own minimal template lifecycle state after the next source task.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-home resolves page template metadata through a provider backed by portal-model source state instead of only static literals.
The provider combines active publication resolution with current template lookup and falls back safely when source state is missing.
Existing scene assembly behavior remains stable for HOME, OFFICE_CENTER, and MOBILE_WORKBENCH.
Tests cover source-backed resolution and fallback behavior without external infrastructure.

tasks.title: Expose portal-model active template resolution query
tasks.goal: Provide a source-side read model that resolves the currently active publication together with its template metadata by sceneType and clientType so downstream modules stop reassembling this join ad hoc.
tasks.scope: Add a lightweight application query and REST endpoint over the existing in-memory template and publication repositories without implementing audience matching, page trees, or preview rendering.
tasks.whyNow: portal-home already bridges publication and template state internally, but the source module still lacks a first-class active resolution contract for designer, preview, and downstream consumers.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-model can resolve the active publication and template metadata in one query by sceneType and clientType.
The query returns RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND when the active publication or linked template is missing.
The endpoint reuses existing source-side repositories and the shared web contract.
Tests cover application and controller behavior without external infrastructure.

tasks.title: Add portal-designer widget palette projection from widget-config events
tasks.goal: Make portal-designer consume widget-config source events so it can expose a minimal widget palette and disabled-state projection instead of only template timeline status.
tasks.scope: Add lightweight in-memory palette projection, event listeners, read API, and tests without implementing drag-drop canvas editing or widget schema composition.
tasks.whyNow: portal-designer contracts explicitly depend on portal.widget.updated and portal.widget.disabled, but the module still ignores widget source events and has no component panel state.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-designer consumes portal.widget.updated and portal.widget.disabled into a widget palette projection.
The palette can return active and disabled widget entries with latest metadata.
Repeated widget events update the same projection entry instead of creating duplicates.
Tests cover event dispatch and read behavior without external infrastructure.

tasks.title: Expose resolved source template metadata in portal-home page response
tasks.goal: Include source-side template/publication resolution metadata in portal-home page responses so callers can diagnose which template is currently driving a rendered scene without recomputing it out of band.
tasks.scope: Extend portal-home read DTOs and tests using the existing provider chain without redesigning page regions or frontend rendering.
tasks.whyNow: portal-home now resolves source template metadata internally, but none of that information is exposed in the page contract for diagnostics or downstream UI hints.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-home page responses include source template/publication metadata when available.
Missing source resolution still falls back safely without breaking existing scenes.
Current HOME, OFFICE_CENTER, and MOBILE_WORKBENCH assembly behavior remains stable.
Tests cover both resolved and fallback response branches.

tasks.title: Expose widget-config list query filtered by scene and status
tasks.goal: Provide a source-side list query over widget definitions so designer and downstream modules can read rich widget metadata without reconstructing a palette from sparse events.
tasks.scope: Extend the in-memory widget definition repository, application service, controller, and tests with a lightweight list endpoint using optional sceneType and status filters.
tasks.whyNow: portal-designer now has a minimal event palette projection, but the source module still lacks a first-class list contract for rich palette rendering and scene-aware widget discovery.
acceptanceCriteria: Widget-config can list widget definitions for the current tenant with optional sceneType and status filters.
The list query returns each widget once with the latest definition metadata after repeated upserts.
The endpoint reuses the shared web contract and keeps existing single-widget behavior unchanged.
Tests cover repository-backed application and controller filtering behavior.

tasks.title: Add template-scoped designer widget palette query from widget-config source
tasks.goal: Expose a designer-friendly widget palette for a template scene by reading source widget definitions instead of relying only on sparse widget-config events.
tasks.scope: Add a read-only application service and controller query in portal-designer that resolves template scene type and returns filtered widget definition metadata without implementing drag-drop or canvas placement.
tasks.whyNow: the current event palette projection is sufficient for change tracking, but not for a real component panel because it lacks displayName, sourceModule, dataSourceType, and policy flags.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-designer can return a template-scoped widget palette using rich widget definition metadata from widget-config.
The query filters widgets by the template scene type and preserves widget status information.
Missing template projections still return RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND without breaking existing designer status endpoints.
Tests cover both the template-scoped palette response and the missing-template branch.

tasks.title: Add portal-model default template canvas skeleton and query
tasks.goal: Make portal-model own a minimal page/region/widget-placement structure per template so designer and downstream modules can stop relying on hard-coded static layouts.
tasks.scope: Add lightweight default canvas domain records, scene-based skeleton generation on template creation, a read-only query endpoint, and tests without implementing save/edit commands, multi-page authoring, or placement validation workflows.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model already owns template lifecycle and widget reference boundaries, but it still lacks the page tree and placement source structure that the module contract reserves for it.
acceptanceCriteria: Creating a template initializes a scene-appropriate default canvas with page, regions, and widget placements.
Portal-model exposes the current template canvas through a shared-web-contract read endpoint.
HOME, OFFICE_CENTER, and MOBILE_WORKBENCH templates each return a stable default skeleton matching their current layout intent.
Tests cover application and controller behavior without introducing save/edit commands.

tasks.title: Add designer draft initialization query from portal-model canvas and widget source
tasks.goal: Expose a minimal designer initialization payload that combines template status, scene-scoped widget palette, and current canvas structure so the designer can load a real draft shell instead of stitched ad hoc requests.
tasks.scope: Add a read-only application service and controller query in portal-designer that composes existing template status, widget palette, and portal-model canvas data without implementing save, preview, or publish commands.
tasks.whyNow: portal-designer already has the status panel and component palette pieces, but it still lacks a first-class draft initialization contract for the canvas itself.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-designer can load a draft initialization payload by templateId containing status, widget palette, and canvas structure.
Missing upstream template canvas or template projection returns RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND instead of partial success.
The initialization contract stays read-only and does not introduce draft save side effects.
Tests cover both the assembled success path and the missing-template branch.

tasks.title: Assemble portal-home layouts from portal-model canvas source
tasks.goal: Replace portal-home's remaining static page and region skeleton with a source-backed assembly path that reads page, region, and widget placement structure from portal-model canvas data.
tasks.scope: Adapt the existing page template provider and assembly flow to consume portal-model canvas views, preserve current aggregation card mapping, and keep a safe fallback path without adding personalization writes or frontend rendering changes.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model now exposes a stable default canvas skeleton and portal-home already resolves source template metadata, so the next gap is eliminating the remaining hard-coded scene layouts in the runtime page assembler.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-home derives page codes, region codes, and placement order from portal-model canvas source for HOME, OFFICE_CENTER, and MOBILE_WORKBENCH.
Existing aggregation card payloads still map into the assembled regions without breaking current scene responses.
When source canvas data is missing or incomplete, portal-home falls back safely instead of failing the page query.
Tests cover source-backed assembly and fallback behavior for at least one scene.

tasks.title: Add portal-model minimal template canvas save command
tasks.goal: Introduce a minimal source-side save command for template canvas structure so designer can eventually persist page, region, and widget placement edits back into portal-model.
tasks.scope: Implement a single full-canvas replace/update command with basic validation and read-after-write coverage, without adding diff merge, collaboration, preview, or version branching workflows.
tasks.whyNow: designer can now load a real initialization payload, but it still cannot persist any canvas edits, so the next source capability should be a constrained save path with clear validation boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-model accepts a minimal canvas save command for an existing template and updates the current canvas structure.
The save flow validates duplicate page or region codes and preserves stable placement ordering.
Reading template canvas after save returns the updated structure without introducing side effects on publication state.
Tests cover successful save, validation failure, and missing-template behavior.

tasks.title: Add designer draft save command backed by portal-model canvas save
tasks.goal: Expose a minimal designer-side draft save command that persists canvas structure back into portal-model and returns an updated designer initialization payload.
tasks.scope: Add a write application service and controller endpoint in portal-designer that forwards a full canvas payload to portal-model save, then reloads status, canvas, and widget palette without implementing preview or publish flows.
tasks.whyNow: designer can now load a real draft shell and portal-model can persist full canvas structure, so the next missing link is a designer-owned save path.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-designer exposes a draft save endpoint that accepts a full canvas payload for a template.
Saving delegates to portal-model canvas save and returns the refreshed designer initialization payload.
Missing template or invalid canvas still returns the shared business error contract instead of partial success.
Tests cover successful save and validation failure behavior.

tasks.title: Add designer publish request command over template lifecycle source
tasks.goal: Let portal-designer trigger the minimal template publish flow after draft save, while still delegating actual source-of-truth state to portal-model.
tasks.scope: Add a narrow write command and response assembly in portal-designer that invokes portal-model publish and then returns refreshed designer status without implementing preview approval or multi-version branching.
tasks.whyNow: once designer can load and save draft structure, the next natural step is closing the loop into publish initiation.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-designer can request publish for a template version via portal-model source services.
The publish response refreshes designer-side status and active publication indicators.
Missing template or invalid version uses the shared error contract.
Tests cover successful publish and invalid-version failure behavior.

tasks.title: Add designer publication activation command backed by portal-model source
tasks.goal: Let portal-designer move a published template into active publication state through portal-model so the designer status panel can reflect real online publication identifiers.
tasks.scope: Add a narrow designer-side activation command and refreshed status response over the existing portal-model publication activation service without implementing preview approval, offline management, or audience scheduling.
tasks.whyNow: designer can now save drafts and publish template versions, but it still cannot trigger the publication activation step that actually drives online state and downstream publication events.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-designer exposes a publication activation endpoint scoped by templateId and publicationId.
Activation resolves sceneType from source template data and delegates to portal-model publication activation.
The response refreshes designer-side active publication indicators after activation.
Tests cover successful activation and unpublished-template rejection behavior.

tasks.title: Add designer publication offline command backed by portal-model source
tasks.goal: Let portal-designer take an active publication offline through portal-model so the designer status panel can clear real online publication identifiers.
tasks.scope: Add a narrow designer-side offline command and refreshed status response over the existing portal-model publication offline service without implementing preview approval, audience scheduling, or publication history management.
tasks.whyNow: designer can now trigger publication activation, but it still lacks the inverse lifecycle action needed to complete the minimal online publication control loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-designer exposes a publication offline endpoint scoped by templateId and publicationId.
The offline flow validates that the publication belongs to the specified template before delegating to portal-model.
The response refreshes designer-side active publication indicators after offlining.
Tests cover successful offline and mismatched template/publication failure behavior.

tasks.title: Add designer preview query backed by current draft canvas assembly
tasks.goal: Expose a designer-side preview query that assembles a portal page view from the current draft canvas so the designer can validate rendering before publish without relying on active publication state.
tasks.scope: Add a read-only preview application service and controller in portal-designer that composes current template metadata and canvas into a page preview using portal-home assembly semantics and placeholder card snapshots, without implementing identity-targeted preview or live publication resolution.
tasks.whyNow: designer now supports draft save, publish, activation, and offline flows, but preview still exists only in contracts and has no runnable backend path.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-designer exposes a preview query by templateId with an optional clientType preview context.
Preview assembles the current draft canvas into a page response without requiring an active publication.
The preview response reuses portal-home page view semantics and returns placeholder ready card snapshots instead of failing empty cards.
Tests cover preview assembled from saved draft canvas and missing-template failure behavior.

tasks.title: Add portal template list query for current tenant
tasks.goal: Expose a source-side template collection query so portal management and designer entry flows can browse existing templates without relying on direct repository access.
tasks.scope: Add a narrow list query in portal-model that returns current-tenant template metadata with optional sceneType filtering, without introducing pagination, category search, or write-side status derivation beyond the existing template view.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model contracts and frontend notes already reference a template list capability, but the backend currently only supports create, current-by-id, publish, and deprecate operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-model exposes a template collection query endpoint for the current tenant.
The list query supports optional filtering by sceneType.
Returned templates are ordered deterministically and include the existing PortalTemplateView payload.
Tests cover unfiltered list, sceneType filtering, and tenant isolation behavior.

tasks.title: Add portal-designer template draft list query
tasks.goal: Expose a designer-side template collection query so the template draft list page can browse current-tenant templates without calling portal-model directly.
tasks.scope: Add a narrow list query in portal-designer backed by the local template projection, including tenant-aware filtering and optional sceneType selection, without introducing pagination, keyword search, or derived workflow states beyond the existing status view.
tasks.whyNow: portal-designer contracts already mention a template draft list, but the module still only exposes per-template status and preview endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-designer exposes a template collection query endpoint for the current tenant.
The list query supports optional filtering by sceneType.
Results are ordered deterministically and reuse the existing designer template status view payload.
Tests cover unfiltered list, sceneType filtering, and tenant isolation behavior.

tasks.title: Add portal-model publication list query
tasks.goal: Expose a publication catalog query so portal management and designer status flows can inspect current-tenant publications without enumerating ids one by one.
tasks.scope: Add a narrow list query in portal-model that returns current-tenant publication metadata with optional sceneType, clientType, and status filtering, without introducing pagination, publish-mode filters, or audience resolution.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model contracts already mention publication lists, but the backend currently only supports current-by-id, current active, activate, and offline operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-model exposes a publication collection query endpoint for the current tenant.
The list query supports optional filtering by sceneType, clientType, and status.
Returned publications are ordered deterministically and reuse the existing PortalPublicationView payload.
Tests cover unfiltered list, filter combinations, and tenant isolation behavior.

tasks.title: Add designer preview identity context query support
tasks.goal: Let portal-designer preview run against an explicit target identity context so designers can inspect draft rendering under different preview identities without touching live identity state.
tasks.scope: Extend the existing preview query with optional identity context parameters and an explicit preview identity payload in the response, without introducing live identity resolution, personalization writes, or publication-scope evaluation.
tasks.whyNow: designer preview is now functional, but frontend and domain notes already imply target-identity preview switching while the backend still hardcodes a synthetic preview identity.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-designer preview accepts optional identity-context query parameters.
Preview uses the provided identity context when assembling synthetic card snapshots and falls back to stable defaults when omitted.
The preview response returns the effective preview identity context explicitly.
Tests cover explicit identity override and default fallback behavior.

tasks.title: Harden portal-model draft and published version boundaries
tasks.goal: Tighten portal-model source semantics so draft editing, published snapshots, and concurrent saves have explicit boundaries instead of sharing one loosely mutable version path.
tasks.scope: Add minimal source-side version metadata, optimistic concurrency checks, and published-version read-only guards to the existing template and canvas save flows without introducing approval workflow, distributed locking, or persistence migrations.
tasks.whyNow: designer can already save drafts, publish versions, and control publication lifecycle, but the underlying source model still lacks a clear writable-draft versus immutable-published contract, which makes save collisions and post-publish edits ambiguous.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-model maintains an explicit writable draft versus read-only published version boundary for each template.
Canvas save and related lifecycle writes require a current revision token or equivalent version guard and reject stale concurrent updates through the shared business error contract.
Attempts to modify an already published version are rejected, while publish continues to produce or preserve an immutable published snapshot instead of mutating history in place.
Tests cover concurrent save rejection, published-version read-only behavior, and draft continuity after publish.

tasks.title: Add portal-designer publication list query
tasks.goal: Expose a designer-side publication collection query so template management views can browse published, active, and offline publication records without calling portal-model directly.
tasks.scope: Add a narrow portal-designer read endpoint backed by source publication list data and local designer-facing view assembly, with optional templateId, sceneType, clientType, and status filters, without pagination, scheduling history, or audience resolution.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model already provides a publication list source query and designer already owns template list and publication control endpoints, so the remaining read-side gap is a designer publication list tailored for management pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-designer exposes a publication collection query endpoint for the current tenant.
The query supports optional filtering by templateId, sceneType, clientType, and publication status.
Results are ordered deterministically and return a designer-facing publication view without forcing the UI to join template status and source publication data on its own.
Tests cover unfiltered list, filter combinations, and tenant isolation behavior.

tasks.title: Enforce widget reference status on portal-model draft save and publish
tasks.goal: Turn the existing widget reference status projection into write-side validation so disabled or stale widgets cannot continue to flow through draft save and publish transitions silently.
tasks.scope: Reuse the current reference-status projection inside portal-model save and publish validation paths, surfacing repair-oriented errors without auto-rewriting canvases, batch migrations, or historical publication mutation.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model already tracks widget reference invalidation and exposes it for inspection, but the write path still has no enforcement boundary, so stale widget references can remain embedded until a later manual diagnosis step.
acceptanceCriteria: Draft save and publish validate referenced widgets against the existing reference-status projection before committing source changes.
Disabled, missing, or otherwise unrecoverable widget references are rejected with explicit affected widget identifiers or placements in the shared error payload.
Validation rules distinguish between mutable draft repair scenarios and immutable published history so enforcement does not rewrite historical snapshots in place.
Tests cover save rejection, publish rejection, and a non-impacted update path.

tasks.title: Align portal identity-context semantics across preview, publication, and runtime assembly
tasks.goal: Unify how portal preview, published template resolution, and portal-home runtime assembly interpret identity-context fields so tenant, person, assignment, and preview overrides follow one consistent cross-module contract.
tasks.scope: Align designer preview inputs, source publication metadata, and portal-home/runtime resolution semantics across the involved modules without adding live identity writes, audience scheduling, or end-user personalization changes.
tasks.whyNow: designer preview identity overrides now exist and publication capabilities are broad enough to expose mismatched context assumptions, so the next cross-module gap is making preview-time identity and runtime identity resolution follow the same contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Preview identity parameters, publication metadata, and runtime resolution all use the same required and optional identity-context fields.
Portal-designer, portal-model, and portal-home document the same fallback and override precedence for tenantId, personId, assignmentId, and related context fields.
Cross-module tests or contract-level coverage demonstrate that preview and runtime assembly resolve equivalent identity inputs consistently.
The alignment does not introduce new cross-module writes into identity-context or personalization modules.

tasks.title: Add portal-model publication audience targeting and identity-aware resolution
tasks.goal: Let portal-model carry explicit publication audience targeting and resolve active publications against the aligned identity-context contract instead of treating all publications as scene/client-wide defaults.
tasks.scope: Add minimal audience metadata, identity-aware match logic, and source query/list response fields over the existing publication model without introducing approval workflow, scheduling windows, end-user personalization writes, or a complex rule DSL.
tasks.whyNow: draft version boundaries, designer publication list coverage, and cross-module identity-context semantics are now stable, so the next portal gap is turning publication records into scoped releases with deterministic audience matching.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-model publication metadata can represent tenant-default and identity-scoped audience targets using the aligned identity-context fields.
Active publication resolution accepts identity-context input and deterministically prefers the most specific matching publication over tenant-wide fallback.
Publication list or current-publication responses expose audience summary fields so downstream modules can inspect scoped versus default releases without repository joins.
Tests cover tenant-default fallback, specific audience match, and non-match behavior.

tasks.title: Make personalization and portal-home consume publication audience source truth
tasks.goal: Let personalization and portal-home consume portal-model publication audience source truth with the aligned identity-context contract so runtime base-publication binding and page assembly stop assuming tenant-wide defaults.
tasks.scope: Wire identity-aware publication resolution into personalization base publication binding and portal-home runtime assembly/read paths, preserving source truth in portal-model and avoiding new publication writes, designer changes, or a separate audience cache.
tasks.whyNow: portal-model now owns explicit audience targeting and identity-aware publication matching, but the runtime consumer path still resolves base publications and assembled pages as if active publications were scene-wide defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: Personalization resolves basePublicationId from portal-model using the aligned identity-context fields and no longer assumes one tenant-default active publication per scene when a more specific audience match exists.

Portal-home page assembly consumes the same identity-aware publication resolution semantics as personalization so runtime page selection and personalization base binding stay consistent for the same identity input.
Runtime read responses expose enough publication audience or resolution metadata for diagnosis without duplicating portal-model source ownership.
Tests cover tenant-default fallback, identity-scoped match, and a consistency case where personalization and portal-home select the same publication for equivalent identity context.

tasks.title: Apply personalization overlay to live portal-home page assembly
tasks.goal: Make the live portal-home assembly path actually apply personalization overlay data so widgetOrderOverride and hiddenPlacementCodes change the final runtime page instead of remaining stored-only preferences.
tasks.scope: Limit this slice to live runtime assembly and read responses across personalization and portal-home; apply ordering and hide overrides on top of the resolved published canvas, and do not fold designer preview parity or new save-time validation rules into the same task.
tasks.whyNow: publication audience source truth is now consumed end-to-end, so the next missing user-visible gap is that saved personalization overlay data still does not reshape the live assembled page.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal-home filters hiddenPlacementCodes from the live assembled page after published-canvas resolution instead of leaving hidden placements visible at runtime.
Portal-home applies widgetOrderOverride within the resolved live page scope while preserving deterministic fallback order for placements not mentioned in the override.
Reset or empty personalization data cleanly falls back to the published canvas default order and visibility for the same resolved base publication.
Tests cover live hidden placement filtering, partial order override application, and reset/default fallback behavior.

tasks.title: Align designer preview with personalization overlay semantics
tasks.goal: Keep designer preview structurally consistent with live personalization overlay behavior so previewed page order and visibility do not drift from runtime results for the same identity context.
tasks.scope: Extend preview-side assembly semantics only; reuse existing preview identity inputs and avoid adding live personalization writes, publication changes, or save validation in this task.
tasks.whyNow: once live runtime starts honoring order and hide overlays, preview becomes the next likely drift point unless it explicitly follows the same overlay application rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Designer preview can apply the same placement hide and ordering overlay semantics used by live portal-home assembly for equivalent identity inputs.
Preview responses make it explicit whether personalization overlay semantics were applied or bypassed.
Tests cover at least one equivalence case where preview and live assembly return the same effective placement order and visibility for matching inputs.
Preview remains read-only and does not mutate live personalization profiles.

tasks.title: Validate personalization overlay against live publication placements on save
tasks.goal: Add save-time validation for widgetOrderOverride and hiddenPlacementCodes so personalization cannot persist overlay references that the resolved live publication cannot apply safely.
tasks.scope: Validate overlay codes against the resolved live publication placement set and current allowed override boundary, return explicit business errors, and avoid auto-repairing stored profiles or changing designer workflows.
tasks.whyNow: after live runtime starts applying overlay data directly, stale or illegal placement codes become visible structural defects instead of dormant stored preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: Saving rejects unknown, duplicate, or cross-publication placement codes in widgetOrderOverride and hiddenPlacementCodes against the resolved live publication context.
Saving rejects attempts to hide required placements or otherwise violate the allowed overlay boundary defined by the live template/publication.
Error responses identify the offending placement codes or conflict reason instead of silently trimming invalid overlay inputs.
Tests cover successful save, unknown placement rejection, and stale publication mismatch behavior.
